OKLO.title: Oklo Reports Q1 Earnings, Shares Slide

macro_events.event_summary: Speculation that Boeing may resume/expand aircraft deliveries to China, potentially reopening a major end-market that has been largely closed amid US-China trade tensions.
primary_drivers: Restored access to China market lifting Boeing's order book and delivery cadence
Supplier flow-through on increased 737 MAX / 787 production rates
Competitive share shift away from Airbus in China

beneficiaries.rationale: Direct beneficiary — China historically accounted for ~20-25% of Boeing's commercial backlog; reopening unlocks deferred 737 MAX deliveries and new widebody orders, materially improving FCF trajectory.
Hexcel supplies advanced composites for 787 (widebody most demanded by Chinese carriers) and 737; rate increases tied to China deliveries flow through to volume.
Howmet makes engine components and fasteners with high content per Boeing aircraft; benefits from build rate increases on both narrowbody and widebody programs.
GE Aerospace's CFM JV (with Safran) is sole engine supplier on 737 MAX; more China deliveries means more LEAP engine shipments plus high-margin aftermarket over time.
Collins Aerospace content on 737/787 (avionics, interiors, landing systems) scales with Boeing delivery volumes to China.
TransDigm supplies proprietary components on Boeing platforms; higher OE deliveries plus future aftermarket on installed Chinese fleet.

losers.rationale: Not a direct loser — leaving empty exposure here would be more honest.

headlines: Boeing could finally reignite its China business. Will that breathe new life into its stock?
